channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I optimized my SvelteKit website from 70 to 95+ on mobile Lighthouse tests
item.description: When I initially built this portfolio/blog website, my priority was getting it live quickly, so I didn't pay much attention to performance. The stack I used — SvelteKit and TailwindCSS — handled a...

item.title: Exploring Cross-Chain NFT Marketplaces and Their Benefits
item.description: The NFT ecosystem has expanded rapidly, and collectors are no longer limited to assets created on a single blockchain. Cross-chain marketplaces are emerging as an important development, allowing...

item.title: You Deployed a Service With No Revenue Endpoint: Future of Filmmaking by Renzo Merbis
item.description: You Deployed a Service With No Revenue Endpoint: Future of Filmmaking by Renzo Merbis You Are Running a Service That Scales to Zero You have built a technically sound video production service. You...

item.title: We’re Measuring AI Velocity, but We’re Ignoring AI Gravity

item.description: React developers write a lot of forms. Contact forms. Admin panels. Settings pages. Onboarding flows. And in most React apps, we build them the same way: Write JSX Wire up validation Connect...

item.title: Inconsistency Resolution in Distributed Systems: Versioning
item.description: Modern distributed databases such as Amazon DynamoDB and Apache Cassandra replicate data across multiple servers to improve scalability, fault tolerance, and availability. However, replication...

item.title: I vibe-coded a free German learning app — here's what I actually ended up with
item.description: I've been building LinguStream on and off for way too long, and it started as a simple question: why does the best German learning method (LingQ-style immersion) cost money? The idea is...

item.title: Docker key terms simplified
item.description: Containerization is a must-have skill for DevOps engineers and software developers alike. It is essential for creating robust, deployable applications. In this article, I will explain the Docker...

item.title: The Trump phone was a no-show at the world’s biggest mobile show

item.title: Multi-Stage VOID#GEIST Malware Delivering XWorm, AsyncRAT, and Xeno RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a multi-stage malware campaign that uses batch scripts as a pathway to deliver various encrypted remote access trojan (RATs) payloads that...

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.description: City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.

item.title: Indie games are turning the act of looking into an art
item.description: Adriaan de Jongh and Sylvain Tegroeg did not necessarily set out to create a new genre. But, in some ways, that's exactly what the duo did when they released Hidden Folks in 2017. Hidden object...

item.title: This phone starts fires on purpose
item.description: Until now, most mobile phone companies have worked to ensure their phones won't start fires. (Occasional Samsung devices excepted, of course.) But this week at Mobile World Congress, we found a...

item.title: After Europe, WhatsApp will let rival AI companies offer chatbots in Brazil
item.description: Meta is now allowing rival AI companies to provide their chatbots on WhatsApp to Brazilian users for a fee, a day after the company confirmed a similar decision for users in Europe.

item.title: The Download: 10 things that matter in AI, plus Anthropic’s plan to sue the Pentagon
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Coming soon: our 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now For...

item.title: Marathon is in a sprint
item.description: Marathon is weird as hell - and I mean that in the best possible way. The latest game from Destiny studio Bungie mixes the slick gunplay the developer is known for with a dark, and frankly...

item.title: Meet your new phone away from phone
item.description: Unihertz's booth at MWC was a little out of the way, but those who did find it all seemed to want to pick up the Titan Elite 2. Sure, the cosmic orange color attracted attention, and the QWERTY...

item.title: Claude Used to Hack Mexican Government
item.description: An unknown hacker used Anthropic’s LLM to hack the Mexican government: The unknown Claude user wrote Spanish-language prompts for the chatbot to act as an elite hacker, finding vulnerabilities in...

item.title: Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
item.description: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.

item.title: The MSP Guide to Using AI-Powered Risk Management to Scale Cybersecurity
item.description: Scaling cybersecurity services as an MSP or MSSP requires technical expertise and a business model that delivers measurable value at scale. Risk-based cybersecurity is the foundation of that...

item.title: Iran-Linked MuddyWater Hackers Target U.S. Networks With New Dindoor Backdoor
item.description: New research from Broadcom's Symantec and Carbon Black Threat Hunter Team has discovered evidence of an Iranian hacking group embedding itself in several U.S. companies' networks, including banks,...

item.title: Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
item.description: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Use TernDoor, PeerTime, BruteEntry in South American Telecom Attacks
item.description: A China-linked advanced persistent threat (APT) actor has been targeting critical telecommunications infrastructure in South America since 2024, targeting Windows and Linux systems and edge...

item.title: How Balyasny Asset Management built an AI research engine for investing
item.description: See how Balyasny built an AI research system with GPT-5.4, rigorous model evaluation, and agent workflows to transform investment analysis at scale.

item.title: Microsoft Reveals ClickFix Campaign Using Windows Terminal to Deploy Lumma Stealer
item.description: Microsoft on Thursday disclosed details of a new widespread ClickFix social engineering campaign that has leveraged the Windows Terminal app as a way to activate a sophisticated attack chain and...

item.title: Hikvision and Rockwell Automation CVSS 9.8 Flaws Added to CISA KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Hikvision and Rockwell Automation products to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities...
